description: Provide a fully-qualified filepath to the dataset on disk.
Common extensions to use: .dat, .img, .hsi, .tif, .nitf, .ntf. For complex file formats, this should likely be the metadata file itself and not individual image files which ENVI supports.
See the documentation for what files should be the primary URL https://www.nv5geospatialsoftware.com/docs/SupportedFormats.html.

If a file has any auxiliary files, specify them here. This would include files with the same base name as the url and extensions such as .enp or .hdr file. Auxiliary files MUST be included if they exist. ALWAYS check for header files.

If a raster has more than one dataset present, optionally specify the zero-based index of which image to open. Applicable to file types like NITF, Sentinel 2, or similar where multi-resolutions or images are present in a single file.
Specify a raster from which to generate ROI statistics.
Provide a fully-qualified filepath to an ENVI ROI file. Should end with an ".xml" extension.

If an ROI file has more than one ROI, optionally specify the zero-based index of which ROI you want. The tool query-dataset-with-envi returns an array of matches and their dehydrated versions to help.
Specify an array of one or more ENVIROIs.

Fully-qualified path to the output dataset, default is "!" which indicates a temporary file will be created. Only set this when requested by user. If this is an output parameter, it MUST not be set to an existing file on disk.
Inputs for running the tool. These *MUST* be specified based on the schema. Summary:
This task computes statistics from one or more ENVIROIs and their associated raster. The ROI statistics can be used as input to supervised classification methods.
; Get the task from the catalog of ENVITasks
; Add the output to the Data Manager
